page: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs (24 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is supported by a 22 Gbps baseline bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory and computational capabilities. Its DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only cached bandwidth of 3830.37 GB/sec and strong uncached read bandwidth of 195.99 GB/sec. Computational benchmarks highlight top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench scores, text processing, and image processing. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) contributes to strong performance in extended instruction sets, encryption, and physics simulations. Additionally, the instance shows top-tier efficiency in static web serving throughput and small-model LLM inference, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.12xlarge offers a highly dense resource profile by combining dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, rapid DDR5 memory, and substantial local NVMe SSD storage. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput local operations, optimizing resource-to-cost ratios for data-intensive tasks. The instance is well-suited for workloads such as relational and non-relational databases, static web serving, software compilation, medium-scale LLM inference, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring high local I/O performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in single-core operations, image processing, and static web serving, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database execution. The inclusion of hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX enhances cryptographic and vector calculations. With a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and high-speed local storage, the m8id.12xlarge provides a resource-dense profile that avoids external storage overhead. It is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining high-frequency Intel processors, rapid DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
2850 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier performance in single-core benchmarks and static web serving
Hardware acceleration for AVX-512, AMX, and cryptographic operations
Optimized for databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
